_README: skill-suggest-regressions.json — anchor pairs for ranking regression detection.
Each case asserts that a prompt MUST surface a specific skill as top-1 with
Exit code is non-zero if any case fails — wire into CI or tick.sh.
- min_score = ~65% of current baseline. Leaves room for marketplace IDF drift
without false-positive alarms, but catches a >35% degradation.
- Each case carries `baseline_score` for audit so future tuners see the gap
between asserted floor and what's observed today.
- When upstream marketplace changes shift baselines, edit min_score deliberately;
don't blanket-rebaseline — that defeats the purpose of regression detection.
Two languages, two roles each (covering both tokenization paths):
- 3 Korean+English mix prompts (mixed-path: Hangul tokenizer + synonym expand)
- 2 pure-Korean prompts (Korean-only: synonym expansion as sole bridge)

cases.prompt: verify deployed URL endpoint SSE static asset

cases.notes: Stripe domain anchor — billing/refund/subscription must hit customer-billing-ops.
Lower min_score — short technical prompt, IDF doesn't pile up.

cases.prompt: markdown article writing blog post tutorial

cases.notes: Pure Korean — exercises Hangul tokenizer + ko↔en synonym expansion end-to-end. If this breaks, either the tokenizer or the synonym map regressed.
Mixed prompt — Korean tokens 결제/환불 plus English Stripe/구독→subscription synonym.
Mixed prompt — 쿼리/최적화/인덱스 must reach postgres-patterns via synonym path.
